title: Allure
description: Improves the textures that deserve an upgrade.
body:
# Allure

![title](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a7209ba31fbe2ca3088caccddfdf1ea0b85f8c02_0.webp)

___________________________________________________________________________________________________________

![about](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/35974551d0ca5bf7c03f8738bc15845a38c74a7e.png)

Allure is a work-in-progress resource pack that aims to improve textures that deserve an update, especially after Mojang is trying new styles and keeps forgetting that there are lot of textures that need an upgrade too. This pack overhauls a lot of textures that people claimed need an upgrade. I decided to recolor some of the game textures to improve their appearance.

This pack does not require Optifine or any other additional mods. It's completely vanilla-friendly.

___________________________________________________________________________________________________________

![feature](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/87a6a905d0c08fe7c63e4292a109dd2378c482e3.png)

This pack includes several changes to vanilla assets like dirt, woods, and many more material retextures. This pack also revamps the in-game disc textures. This pack completely revamps iron blockset and resin blockset. (This pack is still in progress)

___________________________________________________________________________________________________________

![faq](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/bf69e35d5d487a4aa0a123e04bd8d213a6306627.png)

> Is this available for Bedrock?
> 
> Ans: Will have a backport.

> Will this receive updates?
> 
> Ans: Monthly, I guess.

> There is a bug, how do I report?
> 
> Ans: There's a discord server where you can report bugs.

> Can I modify it?
> 
> Ans: You can modify it as long as it's not redistributed anywhere.
 
> Can I have that in a modpack?
> 
> Ans: As long as you have credits, yeah.
